subject: Anthropology
title: Language and Gender
description: Learn Language and Gender in one minute.

hook.text: Women apologize more. Men interrupt more. Or do they? Language and gender research has surprising twists.

buildup.text: Early research claimed women's speech is more polite, tentative, and cooperative, while men's is more assertive.

discovery.text: Robin Lakoff's work highlighted hedging, tag questions, and rising intonation as features of women's language.

twist.text: Later research found these patterns reflect power, not gender. Lower-status speakers of any gender use similar forms.

climax.text: Language doesn't reflect gender naturally. It reflects and reproduces the power structures between genders.

punchline.text: It's not about gender — it's about power.

quiz.question: What do modern linguists say about gendered speech?
options: Speech patterns reflect power, not just gender
